programme.title: Great Egg-Spectations
programme.desc: It’s only week two in the test kitchen and the competition is fire! Head Judge Dan Souza is back with Cook’s Country’s Morgan Bolling and Jeannie Mae hosts. The Task examines the cooks' egg techniques. It’s no yolk! Then, the Test challenges the cooks to make comfort food with a twist! Baker and television personality, Bryan Ford, joins the kitchen as the guest judge.

programme.title: A Perfect Blend of Cooks
programme.desc: It’s health week in the kitchen. First, the remaining nine cooks must create a 90-second solo demo with a blender and a mystery ingredient. Then in the Test challenge, the cooks must pick from one of ATK’s cookbooks and create a dish that could seamlessly fit in. Chef and model, Ronnie Woo guest judges.

programme.title: Too Many Cooks in the Kitchen
programme.desc: This week, the 8 remaining cooks must team up in pairs to tackle a media challenge showcasing simple how-tos. Then in the Test challenge, they team up again to create a meal fit for a modern dinner party. Guest Judge, Melissa King, along with Dan Souza and Jack Bishop, will decide which team is on top, and which cook from the bottom team will be leaving the Test Kitchen for good.

programme.title: Un-Boxing Day
programme.desc: This week, the Task challenge has the cooks faced with mystery ingredients picked by ATK's beloved cast. Cooks Country star Bryan Roof stops by to offer his favorite ingredients and help judge the cooks' dishes. In the Test, the legendary Pati Jinich presents our cooks with the unique challenge of creating a dish with only four ingredients!

programme.title: Parties and Holiday Feasts
programme.desc: It’s a party this week at America’s Test Kitchen! The 6 remaining cooks compete in challenges revolving around party food. The Task dares them to make an appetizer version of popular ATK main courses. Then Jeannie recruits the cooks’ own family members to bring the holidays to the Test Kitchen. Chef Joe Sasto joins judges Dan Souza and Julia Collin Davison in an emotional and celebratory episode.

programme.title: Brooklyn Bites London Hot Chicken
programme.desc: Brooklyn Peltz Beckham is mixing it up in the kitchen and making some London hot chicken with the help of Chef Burt Bakman. Shop while you watch: just head to the Amazon shopping app on mobile and type "shop the show" in the search bar!

programme.title: Family Favorites
programme.desc: Tilly gets personal as her mom, Tana Ramsay, reveals a beloved family recipe, Chicken and Butter Bean Stew. Then she takes on a viral sensation, Feta Fried Eggs, before uncovering the secret to a Chinese favorite, Honey Chicken, in this Family Favorites-themed episode.

programme.title: Restaurant Favorites
programme.desc: Tilly is rejoined by reality star Miles Nazaire for his favorite steak frites, shakes up the viral Spicy Vodka Pasta by flipping it to Vodka Martini pasta, and masters crispy salmon skin served with a to-die-for squash puree in this Restaurant Favorites episode.

programme.title: Brunch
programme.desc: Tilly is joined by Scandal actress Ashley Madekwe for a very British morning of sourdough crumpets, puts a clever twist on the viral pancake cereal trend, and serves up smoke during her first time using a waffle maker as she attempts Southern staple chicken and waffles.

programme.title: Healthy Versions
programme.desc: In this Healthier Versions episode, Tilly prepares a hearty and healthy take on mac and cheese with nutritionist Emily English, experiments with the popular cloud bread pizza trend, and cooks one of her all-time favorite Japanese dishes: tsukune chicken meatballs.

programme.title: Grandma's Recipes
programme.desc: Tilly is joined by Ronald Gladden, the breakout star of "Jury Duty," for a trip down memory lane with his family's own cottage cheese loaf and easy no-bake cookies. She tries her hand at design with a floral-decorated focaccia and finishes with a comfy bowl of pozole in this Grandma's Recipes-themed episode.

programme.title: Fussy Foods
programme.desc: Tilly gets fancy with her guest, Philip Khoury, former head pastry chef at Harrod’s in London, making an Earl Grey Pear Pie. Then, puts a lavish spin on the viral sensation Butter Board, before attempting a truly gourmet Thai dish, Miang Tuna Tartlets.

programme.title: Jamari's LGBTQ+ Glam Soiree
programme.desc: Lizzy Mathis joins Jamari as they make Cheeseburger Egg Rolls, Vegan Spring Rolls, and some tasty cocktails!
